# Twitter/X Launch Thread

## Thread: System Design Mastery 🧵

1/ I built the most comprehensive system design resource on GitHub — 25 modules, 450+ topics, 550+ diagrams.

All free, all open-source.

Here's what's inside 🧵👇

2/ 📚 Foundations (4 modules):
• CS Fundamentals (CAP, scalability, latency)
• Networking (TCP/IP → HTTP/3)
• Linux (processes → eBPF)
• Databases (SQL → NoSQL → Redis)

3/ 🏗️ Core Design (3 modules):
• System Design (caching, queues, load balancers)
• Distributed Systems (Raft, Paxos, gossip)
• Microservices (DDD, Saga, CQRS, service mesh)

4/ 🐳 Containers & Cloud (5 modules):
• Docker (14 topics: BuildKit → registries → debugging)
• Kubernetes (16 topics: pods → operators → network policies)
• AWS (41 services from EC2 to MSK)
• Azure (25 services)
• GCP (26 services)

5/ 🔧 Engineering Practices (5 modules):
• Terraform (IaC, modules, CDKTF)
• DevOps (CI/CD, GitOps, ArgoCD, platform engineering)
• SRE (SLOs, error budgets, burn rate, incident mgmt)
• Security (zero trust, OWASP, mTLS, threat modeling)
• Observability (eBPF, Prometheus, OpenTelemetry)

6/ 🎯 Application (4 modules):
• 22 real architecture case studies (Netflix, Stripe, Figma, Cloudflare, Roblox...)
• 19 hands-on projects
• 26 interview problems
• Staff Engineer (strategy, RFCs, executive comm)

7/ 🧠 Specialization (4 modules):
• AI/ML System Design (LLMs, RAG, vector DBs, vLLM)
• API Design (REST, GraphQL, gRPC, OAuth2)
• Testing & Quality (contract testing, chaos, k6)
• Clean Architecture (SOLID, DDD, CQRS, event sourcing)

8/ The format is consistent across ALL 450+ files:
What → Why → When → Mermaid diagram → Hands-on example → Interview questions → Real company usage

No fluff. Just structured, practical knowledge.

9/ Check it out → https://github.com/ajju853/SystemDesignHandbook

⭐ Star if useful — it helps others find it
🐛 PRs welcome — CI pipeline catches broken links
💬 Feedback wanted — what's missing?

10/ If you're a Staff Engineer or aspiring, module 21 covers:
• Architecture reviews & RFC writing
• Technical strategy & migration plans
• Incident leadership
• Executive communication
• Mentorship vs sponsorship
• DORA/SPACE culture metrics

That's a 🧵 on the full resource. Drop a follow if this was helpful!

## Single Post Version

I've been building an open source system design handbook on GitHub.

25 modules. 450+ topics. 550+ Mermaid diagrams. Every file has hands-on examples and interview questions.

From CAP theorem to LLM infrastructure. From Docker to service mesh. From IC to Staff Engineer.

https://github.com/ajju853/SystemDesignHandbook

⭐ Star if useful, PRs welcome.
